uncovered_waiver.reason: OMN-14820 hand-flips node_checkpoint_validate_compute to canonical def-B via the OMN-14781 path (mirrors OMN-14826/OMN-14823): a typed def-B entrypoint handle(request: ModelCheckpointValidateInput) is added and the pure validate() business logic is preserved byte-identical base_ref<->HEAD (git-re-derived by the ratchet). The selected corpus is valid checkpoints only, because validate()'s error branches (absolute path, invalid SHA, phase mismatch, attempt<1) are pre-empted by ModelCheckpoint's own field/model validators — an invalid checkpoint can never be constructed into the ModelCheckpointValidateInput the canonical def-B adapter path hands the handler, so those branches (and the legacy execute() deserialize/guard branches) are unreachable via def-B and remain covered by the existing envelope-dict suite test_handler_checkpoint_validate.py rather than re-driven through this SUCCESS-only dispatch corpus. Equivalence is proven by that git-verbatim validate() preservation plus green handle==validate==execute parity over the corpus, not by a broad branch-coverage claim.
